top_signals.title: Euro-Zone Inflation Slows More Than Expected as Oil Retreats
top_signals.summary: Euro-area inflation eased more than anticipated as efforts toward peace in the Middle East sent global energy prices lower.

top_signals.title: ECB’s Kocher Says the Next Decisions Will Be Either Hike or Hold
top_signals.summary: The European Central Bank’s next policy decisions will come down to either hiking interest rates further or holding them steady, Governing Council member Martin Kocher said.

alerts.title: Malicious PyPI packages give hackers control of Telegram bot servers
alerts.summary: A campaign active since last November has been targeting Python developers building Telegram bots with trojanized Pyrogram forks that allow attackers to read arbitrary files on compromised servers. [...]

alerts.title: The 250-year history of U.S. energy consumption
alerts.summary: Over the 250-year history of our nation, energy consumption has evolved from wood use in the 18th and 19th centuries to today's use of modern renewable, hydrocarbon, and nuclear technology. In 2025, total energy used in the United States was 96 quadrillion British thermal units (quads), up 2% from 2024, but below 2007's record 99 quads. Petroleum was the most-used energy source last year, followed closely by natural gas. Use of renewable, coal, and nuclear energy each made up about 9% of total energy use.
